business.summary: Inadequate digital channel security directly exposes customers to fraud and data theft, risking significant financial losses and erosion of trust in the institution's digital banking services.

requirements.requirement: Ensure all digital banking channels (internet banking, mobile banking, electronic payment systems) maintain 99.9%+ secure availability with documented incident response for any security-related downtime
requirements.rationale: Customers depend on always-available and secure digital channels; any compromise undermines confidence and may trigger regulatory scrutiny from BNM

requirements.requirement: Conduct risk assessments for each digital channel to classify and prioritise security controls based on transaction volume, customer exposure, and data sensitivity
requirements.rationale: Risk-proportionate controls ensure resources are allocated to the channels presenting the greatest business impact

requirements.requirement: Establish a customer communication plan for promptly notifying affected users of any digital channel security incidents or vulnerabilities
requirements.rationale: Timely customer communication is both a regulatory expectation and essential for maintaining trust during security events

technology.summary: All digital channels must implement layered security controls covering authentication, authorisation, encryption, session management, and protection against OWASP Top 10 web application vulnerabilities.

requirements.requirement: Implement TLS 1.2 or higher for all digital channel communications, enforce HSTS headers, and deploy certificate pinning where applicable
requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit is the foundational defence against eavesdropping and man-in-the-middle attacks on digital banking traffic

requirements.requirement: Deploy web application firewalls (WAF) and conduct quarterly vulnerability assessments and annual penetration tests against all internet-facing digital channels
requirements.rationale: Proactive vulnerability identification and WAF protection address the RMiT requirement to protect against common web application vulnerabilities

requirements.requirement: Implement input validation, output encoding, and parameterised queries across all digital channel applications to prevent injection and cross-site scripting attacks
requirements.rationale: Secure coding practices are essential to address OWASP Top 10 risks that directly threaten customer data and transactions

requirements.requirement: Maintain centralised logging and real-time monitoring of all authentication attempts, session events, and transaction anomalies across digital channels
requirements.rationale: Comprehensive monitoring enables rapid detection of security incidents and supports forensic investigation requirements

governance.summary: Formal digital channel security standards must be established, approved by senior management, and reviewed annually to ensure alignment with evolving threats and BNM expectations.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain a Digital Channel Security Standards document covering authentication, authorisation, encryption, session management, and vulnerability management for each channel type
requirements.rationale: A formal standards document provides the policy baseline against which all digital channel implementations are measured and audited

requirements.requirement: Establish a digital channel security governance committee with representation from IT Security, Digital Banking, Risk Management, and Compliance to review security posture quarterly
requirements.rationale: Cross-functional governance ensures security decisions consider business, technology, and regulatory perspectives

requirements.requirement: Mandate security sign-off as a gate in the digital channel release process, requiring documented approval from IT Security before any production deployment
requirements.rationale: Pre-release security approval prevents deployment of digital services with unresolved vulnerabilities

business.summary: Mobile banking applications are the primary customer touchpoint and a compromise would result in direct financial loss, reputational damage, and potential BNM enforcement action.

requirements.requirement: Ensure every mobile banking application release undergoes security testing and obtains formal security sign-off before publication to Apple App Store and Google Play Store
requirements.rationale: Publishing an insecure mobile app exposes the entire customer base to exploitation; pre-release security validation is mandated by RMiT

requirements.requirement: Monitor app store reviews and customer complaints for security-related issues and feed findings into the mobile security improvement backlog
requirements.rationale: Customer-reported issues can surface real vulnerabilities that internal testing may miss

requirements.requirement: Establish a forced-upgrade mechanism to retire vulnerable mobile app versions within a defined timeline after a critical security patch is released
requirements.rationale: Allowing outdated app versions to remain in use extends the window of exploitation for known vulnerabilities

technology.summary: Mobile applications must implement code obfuscation, root/jailbreak detection, certificate pinning, secure local storage, and anti-reverse-engineering protections as mandated by RMiT.

requirements.requirement: Implement code obfuscation (ProGuard/R8 for Android, bitcode/Swift obfuscation for iOS) and anti-tampering controls to resist reverse engineering of mobile banking applications
requirements.rationale: Reverse engineering allows attackers to discover API keys, authentication logic, and encryption implementations

requirements.requirement: Deploy runtime root/jailbreak detection that restricts high-risk operations on compromised devices and logs detection events for security monitoring
requirements.rationale: Rooted/jailbroken devices bypass OS-level security controls that mobile banking apps depend on for data protection

requirements.requirement: Implement certificate pinning for all API communications from the mobile app, with a secure pin update mechanism to handle certificate rotation
requirements.rationale: Certificate pinning prevents man-in-the-middle attacks even when device trust stores are compromised

requirements.requirement: Use platform-provided secure storage (Android Keystore, iOS Keychain) for all sensitive data including tokens, credentials, and cryptographic keys; prohibit storage of sensitive data in shared preferences or plain files
requirements.rationale: Secure local storage is explicitly required by RMiT to protect customer data on the device

governance.summary: Mobile application security standards must be formally documented, mandating specific security features and pre-release testing requirements aligned with RMiT expectations.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a Mobile Application Security Standards document specifying required security features (obfuscation, root detection, certificate pinning, secure storage) with implementation guidance for development teams
requirements.rationale: A formal standards document ensures consistent security implementation across mobile app development teams and releases

requirements.requirement: Require independent mobile application security testing (MAST) by qualified security assessors using OWASP MASVS as the testing baseline before each production release
requirements.rationale: Independent testing provides objective assurance that the app meets security standards before customer exposure

requirements.requirement: Maintain a mobile app security release checklist that must be completed and signed off by IT Security, QA, and the app release manager before app store submission
requirements.rationale: A formal checklist ensures no required security control is overlooked during the release process

business.summary: APIs are the backbone of digital banking integration and open banking initiatives; unsecured APIs can expose customer data and financial systems to unauthorised access at scale.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a complete inventory of all APIs (internal, partner, and public-facing) with classification by data sensitivity and business criticality
requirements.rationale: You cannot secure what you do not know exists; an API inventory is the foundation for risk-proportionate security controls

requirements.requirement: Define and enforce API lifecycle management procedures including versioning, deprecation notices, and timely decommissioning of end-of-life APIs
requirements.rationale: Deprecated APIs that remain active are common attack vectors as they often lack current security patches and monitoring

requirements.requirement: Establish SLAs for API availability and security incident response with all third-party API consumers and providers
requirements.rationale: API dependencies on third parties introduce business continuity and security risks that must be contractually managed

technology.summary: All APIs must implement authentication, authorisation, rate limiting, input validation, and encryption, with regular security assessments to identify and remediate vulnerabilities.

requirements.requirement: Implement OAuth 2.0 with short-lived access tokens and refresh token rotation for all API authentication; enforce mutual TLS for server-to-server API communications
requirements.rationale: Strong API authentication prevents unauthorised access to banking data and services exposed through APIs

requirements.requirement: Deploy API gateway with rate limiting, throttling, and quota management to prevent abuse, denial-of-service attacks, and data exfiltration through excessive API calls
requirements.rationale: Rate limiting is explicitly required by RMiT and is essential to protect backend systems from API abuse

requirements.requirement: Implement strict input validation, schema enforcement, and output filtering on all API endpoints to prevent injection attacks and data leakage
requirements.rationale: Input validation at the API layer is the first line of defence against injection attacks targeting backend systems

requirements.requirement: Conduct automated API security scanning in CI/CD pipelines and quarterly manual API penetration testing by qualified security assessors
requirements.rationale: Regular security assessments of APIs are mandated by RMiT to identify vulnerabilities before they are exploited

governance.summary: Formal API security standards and governance processes must ensure APIs are documented, inventoried, and subject to consistent security controls throughout their lifecycle.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain API Security Standards aligned with OWASP API Security Top 10, covering authentication, authorisation, rate limiting, input validation, and encryption requirements
requirements.rationale: A formal API security standard ensures consistent protection across all APIs regardless of which team develops them

requirements.requirement: Enforce mandatory API documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger specifications) and security review as prerequisites for API deployment to production
requirements.rationale: Documented APIs enable effective security assessment and ensure deprecated endpoints are properly identified

requirements.requirement: Implement an API decommissioning procedure requiring documented impact analysis, consumer notification, and confirmed migration before deprecated APIs are retired
requirements.rationale: RMiT requires timely decommissioning of deprecated APIs; a formal procedure prevents both premature and delayed retirement

business.summary: Weak customer authentication is the most direct path to account takeover and financial fraud, making risk-proportionate authentication essential for customer protection and regulatory compliance.

requirements.requirement: Define transaction risk tiers (low, medium, high, critical) with corresponding authentication requirements, ensuring multi-factor authentication for all high-risk and critical transactions
requirements.rationale: RMiT mandates that authentication strength must be commensurate with transaction risk; a tiered approach ensures proportionate protection

requirements.requirement: Monitor authentication failure rates, account lockout events, and successful fraud cases to continuously assess authentication effectiveness and identify emerging attack patterns
requirements.rationale: Ongoing effectiveness monitoring is required by RMiT and enables early detection of credential-based attacks

requirements.requirement: Ensure customer authentication mechanisms balance security with usability, conducting periodic customer experience reviews to minimise authentication-related service abandonment
requirements.rationale: Overly complex authentication can drive customers to less secure channels or competitors, undermining both business goals and security outcomes

technology.summary: Authentication systems must support multi-factor authentication for high-risk transactions, risk-based adaptive authentication, and regular effectiveness assessments against current threat vectors.

requirements.requirement: Implement multi-factor authentication combining at least two of: knowledge (password/PIN), possession (hardware token/registered device), and inherence (biometric) for high-risk transactions
requirements.rationale: MFA for high-risk transactions is an explicit mandatory requirement under RMiT clause 12.4

requirements.requirement: Deploy risk-based adaptive authentication that evaluates device fingerprint, geolocation, transaction behaviour, and time-of-day patterns to dynamically adjust authentication requirements
requirements.rationale: Adaptive authentication enables stronger security for anomalous activities while maintaining a smooth experience for normal transactions

requirements.requirement: Implement credential protection measures including secure password hashing (bcrypt/Argon2), account lockout after failed attempts, and brute-force detection with automated blocking
requirements.rationale: Credential protection mechanisms prevent the most common authentication attack vectors targeting digital banking customers

governance.summary: A formal customer authentication policy must define risk-based authentication requirements, mandate periodic effectiveness reviews, and align with BNM's expectations for digital service security.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain a Customer Authentication Policy that defines risk-based authentication tiers, MFA requirements for high-risk transactions, and acceptable authentication methods
requirements.rationale: A formal policy provides the governance framework required by RMiT for customer authentication across all digital services

requirements.requirement: Conduct formal authentication effectiveness assessments at least annually, evaluating authentication mechanisms against current threats including credential stuffing, SIM swapping, and phishing
requirements.rationale: RMiT requires regular assessment of authentication effectiveness; the threat landscape evolves and authentication must keep pace

requirements.requirement: Maintain a register of all authentication mechanisms deployed across digital channels with their risk classification, last assessment date, and any identified weaknesses
requirements.rationale: A centralised authentication register enables oversight and ensures no channel is overlooked in security reviews

business.summary: Out-of-band transaction verification for high-value and high-risk transactions is a critical control to prevent unauthorised fund transfers and protect customers from fraud.

requirements.requirement: Define monetary and risk thresholds that trigger out-of-band transaction verification, covering fund transfers, payee additions, limit changes, and other high-risk operations
requirements.rationale: Clear thresholds ensure consistent application of verification requirements and enable customers to understand when additional verification will be required

requirements.requirement: Ensure transaction verification messages display full transaction details (amount, payee, account number) to the customer through the independent verification channel before final authorisation
requirements.rationale: RMiT explicitly requires customers to see transaction details for verification; partial information display defeats the purpose of out-of-band verification

requirements.requirement: Monitor transaction verification completion rates and investigate any anomalies such as unusually high bypass rates or verification timeout patterns
requirements.rationale: Anomalous verification patterns can indicate social engineering attacks or system weaknesses that need to be addressed

technology.summary: Transaction verification must be performed through an independent channel from the transaction initiation channel, with full transaction details presented for customer confirmation.

requirements.requirement: Implement out-of-band transaction verification using a channel independent from the transaction initiation channel (e.g., SMS OTP, push notification, or hardware token for internet banking transactions)
requirements.rationale: Channel independence is explicitly mandated by RMiT to prevent a single compromised channel from enabling fraudulent transaction authorisation

requirements.requirement: Include cryptographically bound transaction details (amount, payee, timestamp) in verification messages to prevent transaction modification attacks between verification and execution
requirements.rationale: Transaction binding ensures that the verified transaction is the same one that is executed, preventing man-in-the-middle modification

requirements.requirement: Enforce time-limited verification codes (maximum 5 minutes validity) with single-use enforcement and automatic transaction cancellation upon expiry
requirements.rationale: Time-limited and single-use codes reduce the window for interception and replay attacks on verification messages

governance.summary: Transaction verification policies must define clear thresholds, channel independence requirements, and customer notification standards for high-value and high-risk digital transactions.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Transaction Verification Policy defining which transactions require out-of-band verification, acceptable verification channels, and the transaction details that must be displayed to customers
requirements.rationale: A formal policy ensures consistent application of verification requirements across all digital channels and transaction types

requirements.requirement: Document and maintain transaction verification flow diagrams for each digital channel, clearly showing channel independence between transaction initiation and verification
requirements.rationale: Flow documentation provides auditable evidence of channel independence and supports security reviews and BNM examinations

requirements.requirement: Review and update transaction verification thresholds at least annually based on fraud trend analysis, customer transaction patterns, and regulatory guidance
requirements.rationale: Static thresholds become outdated as fraud patterns and transaction values evolve; regular review ensures continued effectiveness

business.summary: Weak session management can enable session hijacking and unauthorised account access, directly threatening customer funds and the institution's reputation.

requirements.requirement: Define session timeout values appropriate to each digital channel and risk context, balancing security with customer usability across internet banking, mobile banking, and payment portals
requirements.rationale: Overly long sessions increase the risk of hijacking while overly short sessions frustrate customers; channel-appropriate timeouts balance both concerns

requirements.requirement: Provide customers with visibility of active sessions and the ability to terminate sessions remotely across all digital channels
requirements.rationale: Customer self-service session management reduces reliance on call centres and empowers customers to respond immediately to suspected compromise

technology.summary: Session management must enforce automatic timeouts, secure token generation, session fixation prevention, concurrent session controls, and proper token invalidation on logout.

requirements.requirement: Generate session tokens using cryptographically secure random number generators with sufficient entropy (minimum 128 bits) and transmit tokens only via secure HTTP-only cookies, never in URLs
requirements.rationale: RMiT explicitly prohibits session tokens in URLs; cryptographically strong tokens prevent prediction and brute-force attacks

requirements.requirement: Implement session fixation prevention by regenerating session identifiers upon authentication, privilege escalation, and any change in security context
requirements.rationale: Session fixation is a well-known attack vector where pre-set session IDs are used to hijack authenticated sessions

requirements.requirement: Enforce concurrent session controls limiting the number of simultaneous active sessions per user, with configurable policies for session displacement or blocking
requirements.rationale: Concurrent session controls prevent simultaneous exploitation from multiple locations if credentials are compromised

requirements.requirement: Implement server-side session invalidation on logout, idle timeout, and absolute timeout, ensuring all session state and associated tokens are completely destroyed
requirements.rationale: RMiT requires session invalidation upon logout; server-side enforcement prevents client-side bypass of session termination

governance.summary: Session management standards must formalise timeout values, token handling requirements, and concurrent session policies across all digital banking channels.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain Session Management Standards specifying timeout values, token generation requirements, session fixation mitigations, and concurrent session policies for each digital channel
requirements.rationale: Formal standards ensure consistent session security across all digital channels and provide the auditable baseline required by RMiT

requirements.requirement: Include session management security testing in the scope of all digital channel penetration tests, specifically verifying token security, timeout enforcement, and session fixation resistance
requirements.rationale: Targeted testing validates that session management controls work as designed and meet the standards specified in governance documents

requirements.requirement: Maintain configuration evidence showing current session timeout settings and concurrent session limits for all production digital channel environments
requirements.rationale: Configuration evidence demonstrates ongoing compliance and enables rapid validation during BNM examinations

business.summary: Real-time fraud detection is essential to protect customers and the institution from financial losses arising from increasingly sophisticated digital banking fraud schemes.

requirements.requirement: Establish fraud detection KPIs including detection rate, false positive rate, mean time to detect, and mean time to block, with quarterly reporting to senior management
requirements.rationale: Without measurable KPIs, the institution cannot demonstrate fraud detection effectiveness or identify degradation in system performance

requirements.requirement: Define and maintain fraud investigation and response procedures with clear escalation paths, customer notification requirements, and regulatory reporting obligations under BNM guidelines
requirements.rationale: Timely fraud response minimises customer losses and meets BNM's expectations for incident handling in digital banking services

requirements.requirement: Conduct quarterly fraud trend analysis using transaction data, customer complaints, and industry threat intelligence to inform rule and model updates
requirements.rationale: Fraud patterns evolve rapidly; regular trend analysis ensures detection systems remain effective against current attack methods

technology.summary: Real-time fraud detection systems must employ behavioural analytics, transaction pattern analysis, and anomaly detection with the capability to flag and block suspicious transactions.

requirements.requirement: Deploy a real-time fraud detection engine capable of evaluating transactions against rule-based and machine learning models within the transaction processing timeframe (sub-second latency)
requirements.rationale: RMiT requires real-time fraud detection; post-transaction analysis alone is insufficient to prevent fraudulent transfers

requirements.requirement: Implement behavioural analytics that profile customer transaction patterns, device usage, login times, and geographic locations to identify anomalous activities
requirements.rationale: Behavioural analytics is explicitly required by RMiT and provides context-aware detection that static rules cannot achieve

requirements.requirement: Configure automated transaction blocking or holding for transactions exceeding defined risk thresholds, with real-time alerts to the fraud operations team for review
requirements.rationale: The ability to block suspicious transactions in real time is the ultimate control preventing actual financial loss from fraud

requirements.requirement: Integrate fraud detection systems with transaction monitoring, anti-money laundering, and security event management platforms for comprehensive risk visibility
requirements.rationale: Integrated systems provide holistic fraud detection by correlating signals across multiple data sources and risk domains

governance.summary: Fraud detection governance must ensure detection rules and models are regularly reviewed, investigation procedures are formalised, and effectiveness metrics are reported to senior management.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Fraud Detection and Investigation Procedure covering alert triage, investigation workflows, customer communication, transaction reversal, and regulatory reporting
requirements.rationale: Formalised procedures ensure consistent and timely fraud response that meets both customer protection and regulatory requirements

requirements.requirement: Conduct quarterly reviews of fraud detection rules and models to assess effectiveness, tune thresholds, and incorporate new fraud patterns identified through trend analysis
requirements.rationale: Static rules degrade over time as fraudsters adapt; regular model review maintains detection effectiveness

requirements.requirement: Produce quarterly fraud detection effectiveness reports covering detection rates, false positive rates, blocked amounts, and comparison against industry benchmarks
requirements.rationale: Effectiveness reporting provides senior management and BNM examiners with evidence that fraud detection systems are performing as required

business.summary: Digital customer onboarding introduces fraud and identity theft risks that must be managed through proportionate security controls without creating excessive friction for legitimate customers.

requirements.requirement: Ensure digital onboarding security controls are proportionate to the risk level of the products and services offered, with enhanced verification for higher-risk products such as credit facilities
requirements.rationale: RMiT requires controls commensurate with product risk; a savings account opening may warrant lighter controls than a credit card application

requirements.requirement: Track and report digital onboarding fraud rates, identity verification failure rates, and false rejection rates to assess the balance between security and customer experience
requirements.rationale: Monitoring onboarding metrics enables data-driven decisions about control calibration and identifies emerging fraud vectors

requirements.requirement: Maintain alignment with BNM e-KYC guidelines and Securities Commission requirements for digital onboarding of financial products
requirements.rationale: Digital onboarding must comply with multiple Malaysian regulatory frameworks beyond RMiT, including anti-money laundering requirements

technology.summary: Digital onboarding must implement identity verification, liveness detection, document verification, and fraud screening controls appropriate to the risk profile of the product being offered.

requirements.requirement: Implement automated identity verification integrating with MyKad/MyKAS validation, facial recognition comparison against identity document photos, and sanctions/PEP screening databases
requirements.rationale: Automated identity verification provides scalable and consistent checking that manual processes cannot achieve for digital onboarding

requirements.requirement: Deploy liveness detection technology that resists photo, video, and mask-based spoofing attacks during the facial verification step of digital onboarding
requirements.rationale: Liveness detection is explicitly mentioned in RMiT as a required control to prevent identity spoofing during digital onboarding

requirements.requirement: Implement document verification with OCR extraction, authenticity checks (watermarks, holograms, microprint patterns), and cross-referencing against submitted personal details
requirements.rationale: Document verification catches forged or tampered identity documents that could bypass facial recognition alone

governance.summary: Digital onboarding security standards should define verification requirements by product risk tier, establish fraud screening integration requirements, and ensure alignment with BNM e-KYC guidelines.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain Digital Onboarding Security Standards specifying identity verification, liveness detection, document verification, and fraud screening requirements by product risk tier
requirements.rationale: Formal standards ensure consistent onboarding security across all digital products and provide the governance framework expected by BNM

requirements.requirement: Conduct periodic assessments of digital onboarding security controls including testing of liveness detection bypass resistance and document forgery detection effectiveness
requirements.rationale: Regular testing validates that onboarding controls remain effective against evolving spoofing and forgery techniques

requirements.requirement: Maintain records of all digital onboarding verification steps and outcomes for each customer, supporting audit trail and regulatory examination requirements
requirements.rationale: Complete audit trails of verification decisions are essential for both fraud investigation and regulatory compliance demonstration

business.summary: QR code payments and e-wallet services are rapidly growing in Malaysia and are vulnerable to tampering, overlay attacks, and forgery that can directly cause customer financial losses.

requirements.requirement: Ensure QR code payment flows display complete transaction details (merchant name, amount, reference) to the customer for confirmation before payment authorisation
requirements.rationale: Customer visibility of transaction details is essential to prevent QR code substitution and overlay attacks from succeeding

requirements.requirement: Establish merchant QR code registration and validation processes to prevent unauthorised or fraudulent QR codes from being used in the payment ecosystem
requirements.rationale: Merchant-side controls are essential because QR code attacks frequently target the generation and display of codes at merchant premises

requirements.requirement: Monitor QR payment transaction patterns for anomalies including unusual merchant volumes, geographic inconsistencies, and duplicate QR code usage indicative of tampering
requirements.rationale: Pattern-based monitoring catches QR fraud that individual transaction validation cannot detect

technology.summary: QR code and e-wallet systems must implement QR validation, transaction encryption, anti-tampering controls, and protection against overlay attacks to ensure transaction integrity and authenticity.

requirements.requirement: Implement cryptographic QR code validation that verifies the authenticity and integrity of QR codes before processing payment transactions, detecting any modification or substitution
requirements.rationale: QR code validation is explicitly required by RMiT to protect against tampering and forgery of payment QR codes

requirements.requirement: Encrypt all QR code transaction data end-to-end from the customer device to the payment processing backend using TLS 1.2+ and application-layer encryption
requirements.rationale: Transaction encryption is mandated by RMiT and prevents interception or modification of payment data in transit

requirements.requirement: Implement anti-overlay and anti-screen-capture protections in the e-wallet application to prevent malicious apps from intercepting QR code display or transaction confirmation screens
requirements.rationale: Overlay attacks are a specific threat called out in RMiT where malicious apps superimpose fake interfaces over legitimate payment screens

governance.summary: Formal QR code and e-wallet security standards must define validation requirements, encryption standards, and anti-tampering controls aligned with BNM and PayNet guidelines.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain QR Code and E-Wallet Security Standards covering QR validation, transaction encryption, anti-tampering controls, and alignment with PayNet DuitNow QR specifications
requirements.rationale: Formal standards ensure security requirements are clearly defined and consistently implemented across QR payment and e-wallet features

requirements.requirement: Conduct security testing of QR code payment and e-wallet features before each major release, including QR code tampering tests, overlay attack simulations, and encryption validation
requirements.rationale: Pre-release security testing validates that anti-tampering and encryption controls work as designed in real-world scenarios

requirements.requirement: Maintain alignment with DuitNow QR security specifications, PayNet guidelines, and Malaysian Payment Systems Act requirements for all QR-based payment implementations
requirements.rationale: QR payment security must meet multiple Malaysian regulatory and industry standards beyond RMiT alone
